data.title: Crypto custodian company BitGo aims for nearly $2B valuation in NYSE IPO. Plans include offering 11.8M Class A shares priced between $15 and $17 each, with an option to purchase additional 1,770,000 shares. Total shares after IPO will be 115,559,776.
data.description: BitGo Holdings Inc., a cryptocurrency custodian company based in California, is looking to achieve a valuation close to $2 billion through its upcoming initial public offering. The company plans to offer a total of around 11.8 million Class A shares, priced in the range of $15 to $17 per share, as disclosed in a recent filing with the SEC. Additionally, BitGo intends to provide the underwriters with a 30-day option to buy up to an additional 1,770,000 shares of Class A common stock. Following the offering, the company will have a total of 115,559,776 outstanding shares, which include 8,855,382 shares of Class B common stock. This move signifies the potential entrance of another cryptocurrency entity into the realm of Wall Street.

data.title: France discovers 90 unlicensed crypto firms; 40% not applying for MiCA license and 30% unresponsive; July shutdowns looming.
data.description: Around 90 cryptocurrency companies in France are operating without a mandatory MiCA license. Of these companies, approximately 40% have no intention of applying for the license, and about 30% have not responded to regulators. Officials have issued warnings of potential shutdowns in July for those operating without the required license.

data.title: Trip.com Group announces its status as a leading global travel service provider with operations in accommodation reservation, transportation ticketing, tours, and travel management. (character count: 152)
data.description: In Singapore on January 14, 2026, Trip.com Group Limited (Nasdaq: TCOM; HKEX: 9961) announced significant developments. As a prominent global travel service provider offering accommodation booking, ticketing for transportation, tour packages, and corporate travel solutions, the company revealed advancements today.

data.title: Registration opens for LA28 ticket draw with 300+ Olympic and Paralympic icons igniting the journey.
data.description: Over 300 Olympic and Paralympic legends participated in a momentous event at the LA Memorial Coliseum, marking the start of the journey to LA28 for athletes and fans alike. The gathering, known as "Ignite," featured esteemed personalities from the sporting world. High-quality photos and videos from the event are available for viewing. Additionally, a high-resolution b-roll footage capturing the LA28 Celebration on the eve of ticket registration opening can be accessed through an external link provided. This event served as a significant kickoff towards the upcoming LA28 Olympic Games.

data.title: osapiens, a sustainable enterprise software provider, secures US$100 million funding from BlackRock and Temasek's Decarbonization Partners. Becomes a unicorn.
data.description: In Mannheim, Germany, on January 14, 2026, osapiens, a prominent enterprise software provider focusing on sustainable growth, revealed a partnership deal with Decarbonization Partners. This joint venture, formed by BlackRock and Temasek, will oversee a Series funding round of $100 million for osapiens.

data.title: December inflation report shows steady prices with CPI rising 2.7%; Fed holds rates, defends stance against political pressure.
data.description: December's inflation report showed that prices were holding steady, with the Consumer Price Index (CPI) rising 2.7% year-over-year, remaining unchanged from the previous month. However, core services inflation excluding housing, known as "super core," softened to 2.76%. Despite this, some experts, like Jeffrey Roach, Chief Economist for LPL Financial, believe that the monthly rate is still "a bit too hot." Roach suggests that the Federal Reserve may hold off on rate adjustments this month but could potentially consider a rate cut by April due to economic risks leaning towards a weakening labor market. Eric Teal, Chief Investment Officer for Comerica Wealth Management, predicts that inflation will likely stay between 2.2% and 2.7%. Meanwhile, strategist Charlie Bilello defended the Federal Reserve's decision to maintain rates amidst political pressure, labeling outside interference as dangerous "price fixing." The market considers the current inflation level "good enough" but not low enough for an immediate rate cut by the Federal Reserve.

data.title: TITAN signs 10-year deal with EPS for 5 million tons of fly ash, boosting ACMs platform.
data.description: Brussels-based company TITAN Group (TITC) is ramping up its Alternative Cementitious Materials (ACMs) platform through a newly inked 10-year deal with the state-owned power utility Electric Power of Serbia (EPS). This agreement grants TITAN access to around 5 million tons of fresh fly ash from the TENT B “Obrenovac B” power plant. This move bolsters TITAN's strategic resource pool for alternative cementitious materials, propelling its expansion efforts in this sector.
